steps.title: Build the transformed function
steps.content:
Given $f(x) = 30x^3$, replace $x$ with $-x$:
$$f(-x) = 30(-x)^3 = -30x^3$$
Adding the constant $c$ gives:
$$y = -30x^3 + c$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept (set x = 0)
steps.content:
$$y = -30(0)^3 + c = c$$
Since $c$ is a positive integer, $t = c \gt 0$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $r \lt 0$ and $t \gt 0$: We showed $r \gt 0$, not negative. ✗
  • Choice B — $r \gt 0$ and $t \gt 0$: Both $r = \sqrt[3]{c/30} \gt 0$ and $t = c \gt 0$. ✓
  • Choice C — $r \gt 0$ and $t \lt 0$: $t = c \gt 0$, so $t$ cannot be negative. ✗
  • Choice D — $r \lt 0$ and $t \lt 0$: Both conclusions are wrong; $r \gt 0$ and $t \gt 0$. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
